title: Implement current open issues by stacked PR lane

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement all currently open GitHub issues (#1883, #1884, #1885, #1886, #1887, #1888, #1893) from current master as coherent stacked PR lanes, then put the computer to sleep after the stack is published.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Start from merged master. Create stacked PRs, one coherent lane at a time, without merging them. Preserve AW mandatory workflow gates. Put the computer to sleep only after local proof, pushed PRs, and final status checks are complete.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact lane grouping, branch names, implementation details, test boundaries, and whether a lane can close multiple related issues.
canonical_core.escalate_when: An issue cannot be closed without redesign beyond the intended lane, destructive system action before PR publication, unavailable GitHub auth, or proof failures that cannot be fixed locally.
canonical_core.next_action: Review the four draft PRs, mark them ready when acceptable, and merge in stack order (#1894, #1895, #1896, #1897).
proof_expectations: Focused regression tests for each issue lane.
AW implement proof selection after each lane's changed paths are known.
Workspace lint/typecheck and relevant CLI test modules before each PR.
Full workspace proof and GitHub checks before review/merge handoff.
completion_criteria: All seven open issues have a stacked PR lane that implements their intended outcome in the ordinary path.
Each stack PR has focused proof recorded in its PR body and remains unmerged.
Final branch stack is published, review-comment follow-ups are pushed, checks are inspected, and remaining action is PR review/ready/merge in stack order.
canonical_core.closeout_decision: implementation stack ready for review/ready/merge in order

goal: Create a bounded plan for Implement current open issues by stacked PR lane.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Implement all current open GitHub issues in stacked PR lanes.
intent.constraints: Do not merge the new PRs. Keep sleep as the final action after stack publication and status checks.
intent.latitude: Group related issues into coherent lanes and choose focused tests/proof per lane.
intent.escalation: Escalate if an issue cannot be safely closed in the current lane stack or if a destructive system action would happen before PR publication.

execution.next_step: Review draft PRs, mark ready when acceptable, and merge in stack order (#1894, #1895, #1896, #1897).
execution.proof: Focused regression, AW implement proof selection, workspace lint/typecheck, and PR checks.

touched: runtime and tests for the current issue lane
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: All current open issues are implemented in stacked PRs and the machine sleeps afterward.
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: yes for implementation, publication, review-comment follow-up, proof, status inspection, and sleep request; PR merge remains the normal reviewer/maintainer lifecycle

required_continuation.activation trigger: reviewer marks draft PRs ready or merges the stack

iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for implementation stack; PR review/ready/merge remains
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: review draft PRs and merge in stack order if acceptable

intent_interpretation.literal request: Merged. Implement all open issues by lane in stacked PRs, then put the computer to sleep.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Start from merged master, implement every currently open issue in coherent stacked PRs, do not merge them, and sleep the computer at the end.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Four stacked lanes: #1888 payload idempotence; #1885/#1887 proof and closeout route; #1883/#1884 startup/output routing; #1886/#1893 PR comment attention and Windows decode fallback.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace runtime/source, GitHub helper script, focused tests, generated/package surfaces if required by proof, planning state/evidence.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Expect up to about 20 files across the full stack, with each PR keeping to its lane.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest per lane; AW implement --changed per lane; make lint-workspace; make typecheck; relevant full workspace tests before final stack; PR checks after push.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing public issue scope, adding a new top-level command, or replacing the lifecycle/proof architecture.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Create a bounded plan for Implement current open issues by stacked PR lane.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.
active_milestone.switch_reason: Switched active lane to Run three long-horizon improvement cycles.

uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py implement --changed <lane paths> --task "Implement all open issues by stacked PR lane" --format json
completion_criteria: #1888 payload upgrade idempotence is implemented and published as stack lane 1.
#1885 and #1887 proof/closeout route improvements are implemented and published as stack lane 2.
#1883 and #1884 startup/output routing improvements are implemented and published as stack lane 3.
#1886 and #1893 PR comment attention/fallback improvements are implemented and published as stack lane 4.
All PRs remain unmerged draft PRs, final checks/status are green, and the remaining action is reviewer/maintainer PR ready/merge in stack order.

execution_run.what happened: Lane 1 (#1888) was published as draft PR #1894. Lane 2 (#1885/#1887) was published as draft PR #1895 and then updated for proof receipt history. Lane 3 (#1883/#1884) was published as draft PR #1896 and then updated to mark task-switch marker matching as a non-authoritative route hint. Lane 4 (#1886/#1893) was published as draft PR #1897, updated to make PR comment fallback portable outside this source checkout, and then refreshed to remove stale planning continuation residue.
execution_run.scope touched: Checked-in execplan closeout state for the published stack.
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py summary --target . --format json; uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_pr_comment_delta.py -q; make typecheck; uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py report --target . --section runtime_mirror_consistency --format json; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: no implementation continuation remains; all four draft PRs are published with review-comment follow-ups pushed and checks green
execution_run.next step: Reviewer/maintainer may mark PRs ready and merge in stack order: #1894, #1895, #1896, #1897.

closure_check.larger-intent status: complete for implementation/publication/proof; PR merge lifecycle remains open
closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The implementation stack is published and proven, but the PRs are intentionally still draft/unmerged and require reviewer/maintainer action.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: PRs #1894, #1895, #1896, and #1897; local proof receipt; green GitHub checks on updated stack heads.
closure_check.reopen trigger: New review comments, failing checks, or merge conflicts before stack merge.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
